Tests object subtraction behavior with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which are used to control how the object behaves in arithmetic operations and string conversions. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation `0`. The code includes an assertion that checks if subtracting the object from `1` and vice versa results in `0`, ensuring that the custom methods are functioning as expected. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on testing the behavior of the object in arithmetic operations.
